message:
fix(codegen): Save/restore func_local_vars for nested classes

When generating nested classes inside methods (like `class X:` inside
a test method), the class method generation clears func_local_vars.
This caused local variables from the parent scope (like `x` from
`x = X()`) to be lost, making needsVMFallback return true and
generating runtime.eval() fallback for attribute access.

Added func_local_vars to the save/restore logic in genClassDef to
preserve parent scope local variables during nested class generation.

This also fixes genFunctionDef for nested functions (def f inside def g)
to save/restore func_local_vars so parent scope variables are preserved.

Fixes test_bool_called_at_least_once which uses:
- Nested class X with __bool__ inside test method
- Local variable x = X()
- Assertion self.assertGreaterEqual(x.count, 1)

Previously x.count was compiled as runtime.eval("x.count") which can't
access the local variable. Now it's compiled as direct attribute access.
